get.summary:
Resolve a user UUID to the user's *current* handle. Frontend uses
this to redirect from durable URLs (`/users/by-id/<uuid>`) to the
canonical handle URL.
Public profile by handle.

parameters.name: label_slug
parameters.description:
Filter the listing to articles bearing this editorial label slug
(e.g. `featured`, `high-quality`). Single label per request — no
AND-ing across labels in v1.

AboutThisTextResponse.description:
Resolved bibliographic info for the "About this text" panel.

Walks ancestors of the active toc node looking for the deepest
`toc_nodes.source_id` and falls back to `books.source_id`. For
Bible-shape works this lets the panel show info about the
constituent book the reader is in (Genesis, Romans); for plain
works (Kant) it always falls back to the hosted book's source.

about_text.description:
Editor's note for this hosted edition (free-form plain text).
Mirrors `books.about_text` of the hosted book the reader is in
(NOT walked across toc ancestors).

resolved_from.description:
`"node"` when an ancestor toc_node supplied a source_id (Bible
books-within-books); `"book"` when the resolver fell back to
the hosted book's root source.

oneOf.description:
Hosted source-language book, when this source is a translation
and the original is also hosted (Kant EN → Kant DE). Absent for
Bible-shape works whose originals aren't hosted.

revoked_labels.description:
Labels whose `revokes_on_edit` flag is `true` and which were
stripped by the most recent author edit. Empty unless this
response is the result of a markdown update that revoked chips.
Frontend uses this to toast the author.

author_handle.description:
Current handle of the author. Use `/users/<handle>` for the
canonical link, or `/users/by-id/<author_user_id>` for a
rename-durable link.

author_public_roles.description: Public-facing role chips for the author (`editor`, paid tiers).

labels.description:
Editor/admin-applied editorial labels. Empty for drafts and for
articles no editor has chipped. Ordered by `editorial_labels.sort_order`.

parent_node_label.description:
Label of the cited node's parent in the toc tree, when one
exists. For bibles this is the bible-book ("Romans"); for
Hegel/Kant it's a chapter title or compilation header. The
frontend uses this together with `reference_label` to render
bible-style attributions like "Romans 13:2".

sibling_translations.description:
Sibling translations: every other book that shares this book's
translation root. Populated when the work has no hosted source
language (Bible: KJV book has [WEB]) so the reader's flat
translation picker can list peer translations regardless of
source/translation polarity. Empty for Kant-style works where
`source_book_slug` and `translations` already express the
relationship. (PLAN_BIG_BOOKS.md Q6)

source_book_slug.description:
The hosted source-language book this book translates from
(Kant EN → Kant DE). Absent when there is no hosted source —
e.g. Bible translations from Hebrew/Greek that we don't host.

translations.description:
Books translated from THIS book. Populated for source-language
books (Kant DE → [Kant EN]); empty for translation leaves.

BookPill.description:
One entry in a Bible-shape group's primary pill row.
`node_slug` is the toc-node slug (translation-invariant by import
guard) — the frontend composes the URL as
`/books/<active-translation>/<node_slug>`.

client_secret.description: Pass to the Stripe Embedded Checkout React component.

url.description: Optional. Full URL (path + query) the user was on when submitting.

EditorialLabelResponse.description:
Public-facing editorial label. `applied_by`/`applied_at` are
deliberately not exposed — readers don't need to know which editor
chipped an article or when.

`revokes_on_edit` IS exposed so the article editor can warn authors
before they make changes that would strip the chip; it's a property
of the label itself, not user-specific or sensitive.

oneOf.description:
`None` when the submitter's account has been deleted; the admin UI
renders "User deleted" in that case.

LibraryGroup.description:
A library entry as the index renders it: a heading plus a list of
works under it. The heading is either an author (`primary_kind =
"author"`) or the work itself (`primary_kind = "self"` — used for
authorless top-level works, including compilations like the Bible
and singletons like Gilgamesh).

book_pills.description:
"Bible-shape" navigation: when this group is a compilation that is
available in multiple translations, the frontend renders the
compilation's child works (e.g. Genesis, John) as primary book
pills here, and the multiple translations collapse into a single
subtle translation chooser. Empty for regular author groups and
for compilations available in a single translation.

id.description:
Stable identifier. Person id for `"author"` groups; source id
for `"self"` groups. UUIDs are globally unique so the id is
safe to use as a React key without a kind prefix.

primary_kind.description:
`"author"` — heading is a person; `books` are works by them.
`"self"` — heading IS the work; `books` are its child sources
(compilation case) or empty (singleton case). Click the
heading to open the work in the reader.

primary_slug.description:
Reader path for `primary_kind = "self"` headings (e.g.
`/books/king-james-bible`). Absent for author headings until
per-author pages exist.

node_slug.description:
For nested works (a child source hosted inside a compilation):
the toc-node slug to deep-link to. Absent for top-level books.

anchor_main_sentence_number.description:
For footnote-kind anchors: the body sentence number the footnote is
attached to. None for body-kind anchors.

url.description: Stripe-hosted Customer Portal URL; redirect the browser here.

handle_changed_at.description:
RFC3339 timestamp of the most recent handle change. Used by the
frontend to compute remaining cooldown days.

sort_name.description: Bibliography sort key, "Last, First" form. Auto-derived at signup.

PublicProfileResponse.description:
Public-facing user profile, returned by `GET /api/users/{handle}`.
Distinct from `ProfileResponse` (the self-edit shape under
`GET /api/auth/profile`) because it strips email and operational fields.

articles.description: First page of the user's published articles, newest first.

public_roles.description:
Roles to render as chips: filtered server-side to public-facing
roles only (excludes `admin` and the default `user`).

anchor_source_ref.description:
`toc_nodes.source_ref` of the anchor — translation-invariant
(e.g. `"genesis:5"`). Used as the chapter-grouping key for
verse-level visual marker projection.

anchor_verse_end.description:
Verse `ref_value` of the end anchor sentence (only when the
quotation spans multiple verses; otherwise null and the
quotation is treated as covering a single verse).

anchor_verse_start.description:
Verse `ref_value` of the start anchor sentence (e.g. `"5:1"`).
Populated when the book has a `verse` reference system; null
for books without verse-style markers (Kant). Together with
`anchor_source_ref` this is the cross-translation marker key.

book_slug.description:
Slug of the book this quotation lives in. Used to render the
translation badge ("KJV"/"WEB" — see `translation_label`) and
to differentiate cross-translation peer quotations from
own-book ones in the reader. Optional for backward-compat
across endpoints that haven't been wired yet; populated by
`list_quotations_for_node`.

projected_source_ref.description:
Target-local source_ref where the projection marker should
render. For same-book quotes equals `anchor_source_ref`. For
cross-translation projection through `cross_translation_alignments`
this resolves to the target chapter (e.g. WEB Rom 14:24 quote
projects to KJV Rom 16:25 → projected_source_ref = "romans:16").

projected_verse_end.description:
Target-local verse `ref_value` for the end of the projection.
Equals `projected_verse_start` when only one end of a range
projects into the target chapter (e.g. cross-chapter spans).

projected_verse_start.description:
Target-local verse `ref_value` for the start of the projection.
Frontend uses `(projected_source_ref, projected_verse_start)`
as the marker placement key, falling back to `anchor_*` when
the projection fields are absent (Kant/no-verse-system books).

translation_label.description:
Short display label for the translation badge — derived from
the source's `publisher` field (which the Bible importer sets
to "KJV"/"WEB"). Falls back to the book title when absent.

anchor_main_sentence_number.description:
For footnote-kind anchors: the body sentence number the footnote is
attached to. None for body-kind anchors.

book_title.description:
Title of the cited bibliographic work (Shape 3): the effective
source resolved for the quotation's anchor — either the per-book
child source within a compilation (e.g. "Genesis") or the hosted
text's root source for non-compilations (e.g. "Critique of Pure
Reason").

has_source_view.description:
`true` when the cited book is a translation AND the original work
is also a hosted text (i.e. there's a `books` row for the
`translation_of_id` source). Used by the quotation embed picker
to decide whether the "Source" / "Both" display modes are
meaningful — for translations whose original isn't hosted (e.g.
Bible translations under the canonical "The Bible" root), only
"Translation" mode makes sense.

parent_compilation_title.description:
When the cited source is a child of a compilation, the compilation's
display title. Frontend renders as "[book_title] (in: [parent])".

translation_label.description:
Compact translation badge — the source's `publisher` when short
(e.g. "KJV"/"WEB" for Bible) or the language code (e.g. "DE"/"EN"
for Kant). Used in My Quotations / reader badge UI to show
which translation a quotation belongs to.

reference_label.description:
Inline reference marker for this sentence when one applies
(e.g. Bible verse `"13:2"`). When present, the quotation card
renders attribution as `Parent ref` (e.g. `Romans 13:2`) rather
than `Book · Chapter · s. N`. Only the first verse-system
marker is returned; other reference systems are out of scope
for this surface.

anchor_main_sentence_number.description:
For footnote-kind anchors: the body sentence number the footnote
is attached to. None for body-kind anchors.

oneOf.description: Omit either field to leave it unchanged.

handle.description:
Optional. If present and different from current, attempts a rename
— subject to charset, reservation, and 30-day cooldown rules.

sort_name.description: Optional. Whitespace-only or empty resets to the auto-derived form.
